constraints: Proposal only; do not implement CLI changes in this commit.
Any implementation requires a separate admission warrant.
The graph-neighborhood path must remain disabled by default.
The target artifact ID must be explicit when graph-neighborhood candidates are enabled.
Use existing discovered artifacts and authoritative artifact-lineage relations only in this first slice.
Do not add external edge-record or future-edge-seed file input.
Do not modify ask_provider.py, compare_providers.py, provider prompts, or prompt rendering.
Do not change default context selection or enable automatic graph-local inclusion.
Do not persist graph state or add retrieval, embeddings, reranking, production index mutation, memory-store mutation, or runtime-manifest mutation.
Keep GAP-0002 open unless a separate future closure verification supports closing it.
expected_outputs: A default-off build_context_pack.py graph-neighborhood CLI surface.
Argument validation requiring explicit enablement and target ID.
Forwarding of optional maximum depth and graph token budget to build_latest_context_manifest.
Tests for unchanged defaults, valid forwarding, invalid option combinations, and repository-lineage smoke behavior.
No provider entry-point or prompt changes.
mitigation: Limit the implementation plan to scripts/build_context_pack.py and its tests, require explicit target selection, preserve defaults, reject invalid option combinations, and prohibit provider or persistence changes.
next_action: Seek a separate admission warrant for this bounded CLI-only exposure plan. Do not implement or expose CAP-0009 through providers until separately governed.
non_goals: External edge-record or future-edge-seed input files.
Retrieval, embeddings, reranking, or index mutation.
objective: Make the existing CAP-0009 default-off graph-neighborhood context candidate path usable from the standalone context-pack CLI without exposing it to provider entry points or changing default context selection.
proposed_change: Add explicit default-off graph-neighborhood options to scripts/build_context_pack.py: an enable flag, a required target artifact ID when enabled, optional maximum depth, and optional graph-neighborhood token budget. Wire those values to build_latest_context_manifest using the already discovered artifact records and their authoritative artifact-lineage relations. Do not add external edge-file or future-edge-seed input in this first exposure slice.
rationale: CAP-0009 already implements and tests opt-in graph-neighborhood context candidates at the library boundary.
scripts/build_context_pack.py currently exposes no CAP-0009 parameters, so the implemented path is reachable only through direct Python calls.
Repository artifact-lineage relations already produce useful authoritative neighborhoods without requiring a new edge-file contract.
The standalone context-pack CLI is the narrowest observable exposure surface and avoids provider prompt or provider execution changes.
GAP-0002 is broader than CLI exposure and must remain open after this slice.
risk: A CLI flag could be mistaken for full graph-local memory selection or could silently broaden provider behavior. The slice must remain standalone, explicit, default-off, lineage-only, and outside provider prompt construction.
scope: Propose explicit build_context_pack.py options for graph-neighborhood enablement, target ID, maximum depth, and graph token budget.
Define parser dependency and validation behavior for the new options.
Preserve current CLI output formats and existing defaults.
Require tests proving disabled behavior is unchanged and enabled behavior forwards only explicit values.
Require a CLI smoke test using repository artifact-lineage relations without additional edge inputs.
success_criteria: The proposal validates through the self-model schema and is indexed as proposed.
The proposal commit changes only self-model records, generated projection, and proposal-specific tests.
No CLI implementation occurs before a separate admission warrant.
After admitted implementation, build_context_pack.py remains behaviorally unchanged when graph-neighborhood options are absent.
Explicit enablement with a valid target ID adds CAP-0009 candidates and diagnostics using repository artifact lineage.
Maximum depth and graph token budget are validated and forwarded deterministically.
Provider entry points and prompt rendering remain unchanged.
Focused tests, the full repository suite, and the self-model audit pass.
summary: Propose a governed, default-off build_context_pack.py exposure of CAP-0009 using an explicit target artifact ID and optional depth and token-budget controls, while preserving existing defaults and keeping provider paths and GAP-0002 unchanged.
title: Expose graph-neighborhood candidates through context-pack CLI
